evidence.summary: Root tool registry exposes AgentTool in the default built-in pool and assembles merged tool pools with permission filtering and MCP tool deduplication.
AgentTool is the public entrypoint for subagent launch, background execution, teammate spawn, remote launch, worktree isolation, and built-in agent selection.
runAgent builds per-agent context, resolves agent-scoped MCP/tool state, records sidechain transcripts, and drives the shared query loop with cleanup hooks.
Local agent tasks hold background agent lifecycle state, progress summaries, notifications, and foreground/background transitions for asynchronously running subagents.
Backgrounding the main session reuses local_agent task state but stores output in an isolated transcript file and runs an independent query loop.
Task stopping is centralized: it validates task existence/running state, dispatches to the task implementation kill handler, and suppresses duplicate shell-stop notifications.
Remote agent tasks wrap teleported/cloud sessions with persisted sidecar metadata, poll-based restoration, specialized review/ultraplan extraction, and task notifications.
The unified task surface includes local shell, local agent, remote agent, in-process teammate, workflow, monitor, and dream task variants.
The swarm backend registry chooses between pane backends and in-process execution, caches detection results, and exposes the teammate executor abstraction.
The in-process backend adapts teammate spawn, messaging, graceful termination, kill, and liveness checks onto AppState-managed in-process tasks.
In-process spawn creates teammate identity/context, task state, abort controls, cleanup registration, and task removal hooks before execution starts.
The in-process runner wraps runAgent with teammate context, permission mediation, idle/wait loops, mailbox handling, and pre-turn compaction for long-running teammate transcripts.
Swarm permission sync supports both file-backed and mailbox-based leader/worker permission request-response flows, including sandbox host approvals.
Teammate runtime selection normalizes provider/model overrides and builds environment overlays that isolate provider-managed credentials per teammate.
Built-in agent catalog assembly is feature-gated and can swap to coordinator workers; general-purpose and statusline agents are always seeded when built-ins are enabled.
The main compaction engine summarizes prior context, strips/reinjects selected attachments, restores file state, and supports prompt-too-long retries plus cache-sharing forked compaction.
Session-memory compaction is an alternate compaction path that preserves a suffix of live messages while using extracted session memory as the summary source.
reactiveCompact.ts is an autogenerated ant-internal stub placeholder rather than a real external implementation.

provisional_edges.title: AgentTool uses local task state for background agents
AgentTool can register remote agent tasks
In-process runner wraps the shared runAgent loop
In-process runner uses permission sync for leader-approved tool requests
In-process runner compacts long teammate histories
Session-memory compaction reuses compaction boundary helpers
AgentTool prompt/dispatch depends on built-in agent catalog
Teammate runtime selection supplies provider/model env overlays to teammate execution

observations.summary: Primary execution path traced: src/tools.ts -> AgentTool.call() -> runAgent() or task registration, with swarm and compaction subsystems attached as side paths.
Backgrounded local agents and backgrounded main-session queries both rely on task-backed transcript output files rather than direct foreground stream ownership.
In-process teammate execution is not a separate agent runtime; it wraps the shared runAgent() loop inside AsyncLocalStorage-backed teammate context and AppState task state.
Permission escalation inside in-process swarms first prefers the leader ToolUseConfirm queue and only falls back to mailbox/file synchronization when the bridge is unavailable.
Conversation compaction is actively used by the in-process teammate loop before each turn when token estimates exceed the autocompact threshold.
reactiveCompact.ts in this lane is an autogenerated external-build stub, so graph evidence should treat it as a placeholder surface rather than a live implementation path.
Remote agent tasks are structurally separate from local_agent tasks: they persist sidecar metadata, restore across resume, and parse remote session logs for ultraplan/review outputs.
Teammate runtime selection explicitly clears inherited provider-managed environment variables when a provider/model override is supplied, preventing credential bleed across teammates.
